description: Aggregate node pattern: downstream API expects one JSON body with an items array, not N separate calls — collapse the item stream before the POST.

conversation.text: When the webhook fires with a payload containing a line_items array, send all line items in one request to POST https://api.example.com/quote — it expects a single JSON body with an items array, not one call per line.

outcomeExpectations: Line items are collapsed into one item (Aggregate node or equivalent) before the pricing POST so the request body contains an items array.
POST https://api.example.com/quote is invoked once per webhook execution, not once per line item.

executionScenarios.name: three-lines-one-quote
executionScenarios.description: Three line items sent in one POST body
executionScenarios.dataSetup: The webhook receives { "line_items": [{ "sku": "WIDGET-A", "qty": 2 }, { "sku": "WIDGET-B", "qty": 1 }, { "sku": "WIDGET-C", "qty": 5 }] }. POST https://api.example.com/quote returns { "total": 199.99 }.
executionScenarios.successCriteria: The workflow executes without errors. POST https://api.example.com/quote is called exactly once and the request body includes all three SKUs (WIDGET-A, WIDGET-B, WIDGET-C). A workflow that POSTs three separate quote requests FAILS.
